source_paper_title: Asymptotic Nash Equilibria of Finite-State Ergodic Markovian Mean Field Games
context:
Section 4.1, Remark 4.6 (page 14 in the provided parsed text pagination)

This paper does not address the convergence of exact Nash equilibria to mean field equilibria. Our method relies on the Lipschitz continuity of \(U_0\), the solution to the master equation. While the structure of the Nash system resembles that of the master equation, connecting the two falls outside the scope of this work. The first author is actively working on this problem, which remains open (see [8]).
title: Convergence of exact Markovian Nash equilibria in finite-state ergodic games to the mean field equilibrium as the number of players grows
latex:
Given an integer \(d\ge 2\), let \([d]=\{1,\dots,d\}\) be a finite state space and fix constants \(0<a_\ell<a_u<\infty\). For each \(n\ge 2\), consider an \(n\)-player symmetric continuous-time game in which player \(i\in\{1,\dots,n\}\) has state \(X^{n,i}_t\in[d]\) and chooses Markovian transition rates \(\alpha^{n,i}(t,x)=(\alpha^{n,i}_y(t,x))_{y\in[d]}\) when the joint state is \(x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\in[d]^n\), subject to \(\alpha^{n,i}_y(t,x)\in[a_\ell,a_u]\) for all \(y\ne x_i\) and \(\alpha^{n,i}_{x_i}(t,x)=-\sum_{y\ne x_i}\alpha^{n,i}_y(t,x)\). Let \(f:[d]\times\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) and \(F:[d]\times\mathcal{P}([d])\to\mathbb{R}\) be given running cost functions, where \(\mathcal{P}([d])\) is the probability simplex on \([d]\). For a Markovian strategy profile \(\alpha^n=(\alpha^{n,1},\dots,\alpha^{n,n})\), define the empirical measure of the other players for player \(i\) by
\[\mu^{n,i}_t:=\frac{1}{n-1}\sum_{j\ne i}\delta_{X^{n,j}_t}\in\mathcal{P}([d]).\]
Player \(i\)'s ergodic cost is
\[J^{n,i}(\alpha^n):=\limsup_{T\to\infty}\frac{1}{T}\,\mathbb{E}\Big[\int_0^T \big(f(X^{n,i}_t,\alpha^{n,i}(t,X^n_t))+F(X^{n,i}_t,\mu^{n,i}_t)\big)\,dt\Big].\]
A Markovian Nash equilibrium is a profile \(\alpha^{n,*}\) such that for every \(i\) and every admissible Markovian deviation \(\beta^{n,i}\),
\[J^{n,i}(\alpha^{n,*})\le J^{n,i}((\alpha^{n,*,-i},\beta^{n,i})).\]
Consider the associated finite-state ergodic mean field game (MFG) with a representative player controlled jump process \(X_t\in[d]\), a deterministic flow of measures \(\mu(t)\in\mathcal{P}([d])\), and ergodic objective
\[J(\alpha,\mu):=\limsup_{T\to\infty}\frac{1}{T}\,\mathbb{E}\Big[\int_0^T \big(f(X_t,\alpha(t,X_t))+F(X_t,\mu(t))\big)\,dt\Big].\]
Let \((\alpha^{\mathrm{MFG}},\mu^{\mathrm{MFG}})\) be a mean field equilibrium (MFE), meaning that \(\mathcal{L}(X_t)=\mu^{\mathrm{MFG}}(t)\) under \(\alpha^{\mathrm{MFG}}\) and \(\alpha^{\mathrm{MFG}}\) minimizes \(J(\cdot,\mu^{\mathrm{MFG}})\).

Problem: establish a rigorous convergence result (and, ideally, a rate) showing that as \(n\to\infty\), any (or a canonical sequence of) exact Markovian Nash equilibria \(\alpha^{n,*}\) of the \(n\)-player ergodic game converges to the MFE \((\alpha^{\mathrm{MFG}},\mu^{\mathrm{MFG}})\). Concretely, prove conditions under which
- the value(s) \(\varrho_n:=J^{n,i}(\alpha^{n,*})\) satisfy \(\varrho_n\to \varrho\), where \(\varrho\) is the MFG ergodic value;
- the empirical measure process \((\mu^n_t)_{t\ge 0}\), \(\mu^n_t:=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{j=1}^n\delta_{X^{n,j}_t}\), under \(\alpha^{n,*}\) converges (e.g., in law, or uniformly in time in an appropriate topology) to \(\mu^{\mathrm{MFG}}(t)\) (or to the stationary MFE \(\bar\mu\) in the stationary setting);
- and/or the equilibrium feedback controls \(\alpha^{n,*}\) converge (in a suitable sense) to \(\alpha^{\mathrm{MFG}}\), relating the finite-\(n\) Nash system to the ergodic master equation.
